title: ranunculus × lacerus subsp. valesiacus
intro: Ranunculus × lacerus subsp. valesiacus is a rare, hybrid subspecies of the ranunculus plant, known for its delicate, pale yellow flowers with five petals. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and other health issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus × lacerus subsp. valesiacus include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
